{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sales Analyst",
  "description": "Zinance Limited is a dynamic Information Technology and Consulting firm committed to delivering innovative digital solutions and strategic advisory services to businesses of all sizes. We combine technical expertise with industry insight to help organizations improve performance, streamline operations, and achieve sustainable growth.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Analyst
Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are looking for a Sales Analyst to join our team and support our sales team by compiling and analyzing sales and market data to help our organization sell more effectively.


Responsibilities


	Collect data from sales reports for analysis
	Develop methods and strategies for assessing large amounts of data
	Study past sales data to determine trends in productivity
	Examine company budget
	Conduct research into competitive companies and the surrounding market
	Study shopping and purchase trends of consumer base and target demographic
	Use sales prediction software to determine future trends
	Create sales evaluation reports based on collected data.


Requirements and Skills


	Proven work experience as a Sales Analyst or similar role
	Possesses strong problem-solving skills
	Communicates clearly and effectively
	Pays close attention to detail.


Salary
N130,000 - N200,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to the Human Resource Personnel via:&nbsp;zinancelimited@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  },

Sales Analyst Job at Zinance Limited




        
    

        
        Zinance Limited is a dynamic Information Technology and Consulting firm committed to delivering innovative digital solutions and strategic advisory services to businesses of all sizes. We combine technical expertise with industry insight to help organizations improve performance, streamline operations, and achieve sustainable growth.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Analyst
Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for a Sales Analyst to join our team and support our sales team by compiling and analyzing sales and market data to help our organization sell more effectively.


Responsibilities


	Collect data from sales reports for analysis
	Develop methods and strategies for assessing large amounts of data
	Study past sales data to determine trends in productivity
	Examine company budget
	Conduct research into competitive companies and the surrounding market
	Study shopping and purchase trends of consumer base and target demographic
	Use sales prediction software to determine future trends
	Create sales evaluation reports based on collected data.


Requirements and Skills


	Proven work experience as a Sales Analyst or similar role
	Possesses strong problem-solving skills
	Communicates clearly and effectively
	Pays close attention to detail.


Salary
N130,000 - N200,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to the Human Resource Personnel via: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Data Manager",
  "description": "Iraizickify Business Store is a reliable ICT and business support service provider specializing in company and business registration under the Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) in Nigeria. We are committed to helping entrepreneurs, startups, and established businesses formalize and grow their ventures with ease and professionalism.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Data Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Create and enforce policies for effective data management
	Formulate techniques for quality data collection to ensure adequacy, accuracy and legitimacy of data
	Devise and implement efficient and secure procedures for data handling and analysis with attention to all technical aspects
	Establish rules and procedures for data sharing with upper management, external stakeholders etc.
	Support others in the daily use of data systems and ensure adherence to legal and company standards
	Assist with reports and data extraction when needed
	Monitor and analyze information and data systems and evaluate their performance to discover ways of enhancing them (new technologies, upgrades etc.)
	Ensure digital databases and archives are protected from security breaches and data losses.


Requirements and skills


	Excellent understanding of data administration and management functions (collection, analysis, distribution etc.)
	Familiarity with modern database and information system technologies
	Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Access, Word etc.)
	An analytical mindset with problem-solving skills
	Excellent communication and collaboration skills.


Salary
N150,000 - N220,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and&nbsp;qualified candidates should send their detailed CV to:&nbsp;hr.iraizickifyltd@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Data Manager Job at Iraizickify Business Store




        
    

        
        Iraizickify Business Store is a reliable ICT and business support service provider specializing in company and business registration under the Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) in Nigeria. We are committed to helping entrepreneurs, startups, and established businesses formalize and grow their ventures with ease and professionalism.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Data Manager

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Create and enforce policies for effective data management
	Formulate techniques for quality data collection to ensure adequacy, accuracy and legitimacy of data
	Devise and implement efficient and secure procedures for data handling and analysis with attention to all technical aspects
	Establish rules and procedures for data sharing with upper management, external stakeholders etc.
	Support others in the daily use of data systems and ensure adherence to legal and company standards
	Assist with reports and data extraction when needed
	Monitor and analyze information and data systems and evaluate their performance to discover ways of enhancing them (new technologies, upgrades etc.)
	Ensure digital databases and archives are protected from security breaches and data losses.


Requirements and skills


	Excellent understanding of data administration and management functions (collection, analysis, distribution etc.)
	Familiarity with modern database and information system technologies
	Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Access, Word etc.)
	An analytical mindset with problem-solving skills
	Excellent communication and collaboration skills.


Salary
N150,000 - N220,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their detailed CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Accountant",
  "description": "Cross Beverage Company Limited -&nbsp;We are a Nigerian startup producing a portfolio of affordable, daily consumed drink products. Our products positions us to meet both everyday hydration needs and the demand for flavored, wholesome beverage in the Nigerian market.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location:&nbsp;Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Cross Beverage Nigeria Limited is seeking a Junior Accountant to lead and optimize our financial operations.
	In this role, you&rsquo;ll ensure compliance with tax regulations, financial laws, and accounting standards while driving the development and implementation of financial policies and procedures that support business growth.
	You&rsquo;ll oversee the preparation and analysis of financial statements, manage budgeting and forecasting, and provide regular insights on financial performance and key risks to the senior leadership team.
	Reporting directly to the Managing Director, you&rsquo;ll play a critical role in guiding the company&rsquo;s financial strategy, optimizing profitability, and ensuring the accuracy and transparency of our financial reporting.


Responsibilities
Financial Management and Operations:


	Prepare, review, and present monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements, ensuring alignment with organizational goals and regulatory standards.
	Develop and manage detailed budgets and forecasts, collaborating with key departments to ensure production targets and financial objectives are met.
	Monitor and analyze production costs to identify inefficiencies and maximize profitability, providing actionable insights to optimize operations.
	Oversee accounts payable, receivables, payroll, and employee reimbursements, ensuring timely and accurate processing of all financial transactions.
	Conduct regular bank reconciliations, closely monitor cash flow, and maintain an up-to-date record of daily financial transactions to ensure accurate reporting.
	Provide financial expertise and oversight in procurement and expense management, conducting rigorous financial checks to ensure compliance and preserve the integrity of financial data.


Compliance and Reporting:


	Ensure full compliance with tax regulations and financial laws, staying up-to-date on changes to maintain the company&rsquo;s legal and financial standing.
	Develop, implement, and periodically review financial policies and procedures to strengthen internal controls and promote operational efficiency.
	Ensure strict adherence to accounting standards, maintaining transparency and accuracy in financial reporting in accordance with IFRS.
	Provide regular, detailed updates on financial performance, highlighting key risks and opportunities to inform strategic decision-making.
	Report directly to the Managing Director, delivering concise and insightful financial reports to guide executive-level decisions.


Requirement and Qualifications


	HND qualification in Accounting, Finance or related fields
	1 - 3 years of relevant accounting and finance experience
	Strong knowledge of accounting principles
	Proficient in Microsoft Excel and accounting software e.g. QuickBooks. Sage. ERP systems
	Ability to work effectively in a fast paced Environment.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;humanresources@crossbeverage.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Accountant Job at Cross Beverage Company Limited




        
    

        
        Cross Beverage Company Limited - We are a Nigerian startup producing a portfolio of affordable, daily consumed drink products. Our products positions us to meet both everyday hydration needs and the demand for flavored, wholesome beverage in the Nigerian market.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Cross Beverage Nigeria Limited is seeking a Junior Accountant to lead and optimize our financial operations.
	In this role, you’ll ensure compliance with tax regulations, financial laws, and accounting standards while driving the development and implementation of financial policies and procedures that support business growth.
	You’ll oversee the preparation and analysis of financial statements, manage budgeting and forecasting, and provide regular insights on financial performance and key risks to the senior leadership team.
	Reporting directly to the Managing Director, you’ll play a critical role in guiding the company’s financial strategy, optimizing profitability, and ensuring the accuracy and transparency of our financial reporting.


Responsibilities
Financial Management and Operations:


	Prepare, review, and present monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements, ensuring alignment with organizational goals and regulatory standards.
	Develop and manage detailed budgets and forecasts, collaborating with key departments to ensure production targets and financial objectives are met.
	Monitor and analyze production costs to identify inefficiencies and maximize profitability, providing actionable insights to optimize operations.
	Oversee accounts payable, receivables, payroll, and employee reimbursements, ensuring timely and accurate processing of all financial transactions.
	Conduct regular bank reconciliations, closely monitor cash flow, and maintain an up-to-date record of daily financial transactions to ensure accurate reporting.
	Provide financial expertise and oversight in procurement and expense management, conducting rigorous financial checks to ensure compliance and preserve the integrity of financial data.


Compliance and Reporting:


	Ensure full compliance with tax regulations and financial laws, staying up-to-date on changes to maintain the company’s legal and financial standing.
	Develop, implement, and periodically review financial policies and procedures to strengthen internal controls and promote operational efficiency.
	Ensure strict adherence to accounting standards, maintaining transparency and accuracy in financial reporting in accordance with IFRS.
	Provide regular, detailed updates on financial performance, highlighting key risks and opportunities to inform strategic decision-making.
	Report directly to the Managing Director, delivering concise and insightful financial reports to guide executive-level decisions.


Requirement and Qualifications


	HND qualification in Accounting, Finance or related fields
	1 - 3 years of relevant accounting and finance experience
	Strong knowledge of accounting principles
	Proficient in Microsoft Excel and accounting software e.g. QuickBooks. Sage. ERP systems
	Ability to work effectively in a fast paced Environment.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development Manager",
  "description": "Contec Global Group - Established in 1984 and headquartered in London, the Contec Global Group has a worldwide presence, with operations in Asia, Africa, and Europe. As a company established on the platform of e-Governance and smart technologies, Contec Global is among the most experienced independent technology and systems organizations operating in developing countries. Today, the Group is engaged, globally, in providing integrated, systems-based solutions in the fields of Secure Technologies, Infrastructure, and Hospitality.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Manager
Job Location:&nbsp;&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a strategic and results-driven Business Development Manager with strong experience in the agricultural sector and proven ability to engage with government institutions.
	The role will focus on identifying growth opportunities, driving partnerships, securing government-related projects, and positioning the organization within public sector agricultural initiatives and policies.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify and pursue new business opportunities within the agro value chain (farming, processing, distribution, agritech, etc.)
	Build and maintain strong relationships with government agencies, regulators, and key public sector stakeholders
	Drive participation in government programs, grants, subsidies, and agricultural interventions
	Develop and execute business development strategies aligned with company growth objectives
	Prepare proposals, bids, and documentation for government and institutional projects
	Monitor government policies, regulations, and funding opportunities impacting the agricultural sector
	Establish partnerships with cooperatives, NGOs, and development agencies
	Negotiate contracts and close high-value deals with both public and private sector clients
	Collaborate with internal teams (operations, finance, and marketing) to deliver on projects
	Represent the company at industry events, stakeholder meetings, and policy forums


Required Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Agriculture, Agribusiness, Business Administration, or a related field
	5&ndash;8+ years of experience in business development within the agricultural sector
	Proven experience working with or engaging government bodies and public sector institutions
	Strong understanding of agricultural value chains and market dynamics
	Demonstrated success in securing partnerships, grants, or large-scale projects
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills


Preferred Qualifications:


	Master&rsquo;s Degree (MBA, Agribusiness, or related field) is an advantage
	Experience with donor-funded projects (e.g., World Bank, IFAD, AFDB programs)
	Strong network within government ministries, agricultural agencies, and industry bodies
	Knowledge of regulatory frameworks and compliance in the agricultural sector

Business Development Manager Job at Contec Global Group




        
    

        
        Contec Global Group - Established in 1984 and headquartered in London, the Contec Global Group has a worldwide presence, with operations in Asia, Africa, and Europe. As a company established on the platform of e-Governance and smart technologies, Contec Global is among the most experienced independent technology and systems organizations operating in developing countries. Today, the Group is engaged, globally, in providing integrated, systems-based solutions in the fields of Secure Technologies, Infrastructure, and Hospitality.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Manager
Job Location:  Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Summary


	We are seeking a strategic and results-driven Business Development Manager with strong experience in the agricultural sector and proven ability to engage with government institutions.
	The role will focus on identifying growth opportunities, driving partnerships, securing government-related projects, and positioning the organization within public sector agricultural initiatives and policies.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify and pursue new business opportunities within the agro value chain (farming, processing, distribution, agritech, etc.)
	Build and maintain strong relationships with government agencies, regulators, and key public sector stakeholders
	Drive participation in government programs, grants, subsidies, and agricultural interventions
	Develop and execute business development strategies aligned with company growth objectives
	Prepare proposals, bids, and documentation for government and institutional projects
	Monitor government policies, regulations, and funding opportunities impacting the agricultural sector
	Establish partnerships with cooperatives, NGOs, and development agencies
	Negotiate contracts and close high-value deals with both public and private sector clients
	Collaborate with internal teams (operations, finance, and marketing) to deliver on projects
	Represent the company at industry events, stakeholder meetings, and policy forums


Required Qualifications


	Bachelor’s degree in Agriculture, Agribusiness, Business Administration, or a related field
	5–8+ years of experience in business development within the agricultural sector
	Proven experience working with or engaging government bodies and public sector institutions
	Strong understanding of agricultural value chains and market dynamics
	Demonstrated success in securing partnerships, grants, or large-scale projects
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills


Preferred Qualifications:


	Master’s Degree (MBA, Agribusiness, or related field) is an advantage
	Experience with donor-funded projects (e.g., World Bank, IFAD, AFDB programs)
	Strong network within government ministries, agricultural agencies, and industry bodies
	Knowledge of regulatory frameworks and compliance in the agricultural sector
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Account Officer",
  "description": "Lily Hospitals Limited, established since 1986 remains one of the foremost hospitals in Nigeria and within the South-South region of the country. It is the first private hospital in Nigeria to achieve ISO 9001:2000 quality management system certification from the Standards Organization of Nigeria (SON) and was also recently accredited with COHSASA (Council for Health Service Accreditation of Southern Africa) Certificate.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Warri, Delta
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are looking for a skilled, reliable, and experienced Accountant to join our finance team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing the company&rsquo;s financial records, ensuring compliance with statutory and regulatory requirements, and providing accurate financial information to support business decisions.


Key Responsibilities


	Prepare and maintain accurate financial records, reports, and statements
	Manage accounts payable and receivable processes
	Reconcile bank statements and monitor company cash flow
	Prepare budgets, financial forecasts, and expenditure reports
	Ensure compliance with accounting standards, tax laws, and company policies
	Assist with monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reporting
	Support internal and external audit processes
	Monitor financial transactions and recommend improvements for efficiency
	Maintain proper documentation of all financial records


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or related discipline with&nbsp;2&ndash;4 years relevant accounting experience
	Proficiency in accounting software and Microsoft Excel
	Strong knowledge of financial reporting and reconciliation
	Good understanding of tax regulations and accounting principles
	Excellent numerical, analytical, and organizational skills
	Strong attention to detail and high level of integrity

Account Officer Job at Lily Hospitals Limited




        
    

        
        Lily Hospitals Limited, established since 1986 remains one of the foremost hospitals in Nigeria and within the South-South region of the country. It is the first private hospital in Nigeria to achieve ISO 9001:2000 quality management system certification from the Standards Organization of Nigeria (SON) and was also recently accredited with COHSASA (Council for Health Service Accreditation of Southern Africa) Certificate.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Officer

Job Location: Warri, Delta
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for a skilled, reliable, and experienced Accountant to join our finance team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing the company’s financial records, ensuring compliance with statutory and regulatory requirements, and providing accurate financial information to support business decisions.


Key Responsibilities


	Prepare and maintain accurate financial records, reports, and statements
	Manage accounts payable and receivable processes
	Reconcile bank statements and monitor company cash flow
	Prepare budgets, financial forecasts, and expenditure reports
	Ensure compliance with accounting standards, tax laws, and company policies
	Assist with monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reporting
	Support internal and external audit processes
	Monitor financial transactions and recommend improvements for efficiency
	Maintain proper documentation of all financial records


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or related discipline with 2–4 years relevant accounting experience
	Proficiency in accounting software and Microsoft Excel
	Strong knowledge of financial reporting and reconciliation
	Good understanding of tax regulations and accounting principles
	Excellent numerical, analytical, and organizational skills
	Strong attention to detail and high level of integrity
	Professional certification such as ICAN or ACCA is an added advantage
	Must reside in Warri, Delta State or its environs


Benefits


	Competitive salary package
	Health insurance coverage
	Pension contributions
	Professional training and career development opportunities
	Structured career progression
	Positive and professional work environment


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Credentials to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
